bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no real time pressure, so I am targeting a very high-utility opening bid rather than conceding immediately. The opponent’s all-valueF offer is very low utility for me, and their urgency message is cheap talk at the start of negotiation, so I will counter with my strongest valid values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still extremely early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid and not treating the opponent’s urgency messages as credible. Their repeated all-valueF offer is very poor for me and shows no concession yet, so I will make only a small move from my prior maximum bid to avoid repeating exactly while holding firm on the highest-value issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-value offers or their artificial urgency messages. The opponent has held mostly at valueF and even moved issueD from valueF to valueE, which lowers my utility, so I see no meaningful concession yet. I will make a small non-repeating concession on issueD while holding firm on my higher-value issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not made meaningful concessions; their offers remain very low utility to me and their urgency messages are cheap talk. To avoid repeating my prior bid exactly, I make a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on the highest-value options for issueA, issueB, and issueD.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession from my last offer. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, though their latest bid moved issueC to valueC, so I am matching that signal on issueC while holding firm on my higher-value positions elsewhere and ignoring the repeated false urgency messages.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low for me with only minimal concession. Their messages about urgency are not supported by the actual time fraction, so I treat them as pressure tactics; I will make a small concession on issueC while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.12 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me with only minimal substantive concessions.
